title: IMGUI Adaptive Layout
use_for: designing adaptation policy for IMGUI layout systems that borrow useful ideas from uGUI without using uGUI components
defining anchor-like placement, alignment, and scaling rules over manually computed IMGUI geometry
preserving readability of diagnostics overlays, dashboards, and tool surfaces across resolution changes
defining size constraints and adaptive scaling policies for windows and widgets across resolutions
rules: treat uGUI only as a design reference, not as an implementation dependency
apply adaptation policy over retained IMGUI layout intent and explicit host geometry
treat anchors, alignment, and scale policy as separate concerns
do not let adaptation policy redefine the composition tree
allow smaller resolutions to reduce effective maximum component size rather than only applying uniform shrink
treat typography scaling and readability thresholds as first-class adaptation outputs, not incidental styling details
procedure: Start from an already defined layout structure.
Define root-host attachment semantics and local alignment semantics separately.
Define scaling rules and size bounds for each major shell region and component type.
Validate that nested layouts and adaptive rules stay deterministic across IMGUI phases.

primary_when: the request is specifically about adaptive layout, anchor-like placement, scaling policy, or size bounds in an IMGUI framework
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about overall IMGUI framework ownership or input routing

solves: how to borrow the strongest layout ideas from uGUI while staying inside an IMGUI-based framework
how to keep adaptation policy separate from composition structure
how to adapt windows and widgets to multiple resolutions without relying on Unity retained UI components

core_principles: Use IMGUI as the rendering and event foundation, but keep layout intent retained and explicit.
Represent anchor-like placement, alignment, and scaling as framework concepts rather than Unity component dependencies.
Apply adaptation only after structure is known.
Adaptation must control both placement and bounded size, not just global scale.
Window shells and compact widgets may share one layout grammar while keeping different size policies.
constraints: The framework must not depend on Canvas, RectTransform, or LayoutGroup components.
Layout intent must remain stable across IMGUI passes even when geometry is recomputed.
Adaptation policy must not mutate structural slot ownership.
Scaling rules must permit reduced effective maxima on smaller resolutions.
Anchor-like semantics must stay simple enough to reason about during review.
decision_rules: Use anchor-like rules for host attachment semantics, not as a substitute for composition structure.
Let imgui-layout-composition own shell and panel structure; use this skill to adapt that structure across resolutions.
Apply scaling after structural layout intent is resolved, not before structure exists.
Constrain windows and widgets through min, preferred, and max size thinking where density matters.
Prefer one shared layout grammar for windows and widgets, with different policies rather than unrelated systems.
Use this skill early for diagnostics UI when readability at 720p, 1080p, and larger resolutions is part of the product constraint.

preferred_patterns: retained layout tree adapted after structural resolution
resolution-aware size policies for shell regions and components
explicit separation of layout resolution and draw execution
avoid_patterns: direct dependence on uGUI runtime components
adaptation helpers that also decide composition structure
uniform scale-only adaptation with no size ceilings
mixing structural layout intent with immediate draw-time side effects

recipes.name: Design a uGUI-inspired adaptive layout layer over IMGUI
recipes.when: Use when the framework needs predictable composition and adaptation without using uGUI itself.
steps: Start from the retained layout tree defined by the framework.
Define how anchor-like placement and local alignment apply to that tree.
Apply adaptive scaling and size bounds per region or component category.
Feed the resulting geometry into deterministic IMGUI draw passes.

pitfalls: Treating uGUI as a runtime dependency instead of a conceptual reference.
Collapsing anchors, alignment, sizing, and scaling into one opaque layout helper.
Letting adaptation logic rewrite structural composition rules.
Leaving size ceilings undefined so small resolutions break readability.
Encoding adaptation only in feature-specific Rect math with no shared policy.
review_checklist: Is uGUI treated only as inspiration rather than dependency?
Is adaptation policy clearly separated from composition structure?
Are anchors, alignment, and scale policy separated clearly?
Are font scaling and readability thresholds explicit rather than implicit?
Can lower resolutions reduce effective maximum component size?
Can the same composition tree survive across resolutions with only policy changes?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI uGUI-Inspired Composition
references.interpretation: Use this first for the domain's stance that IMGUI remains the implementation base while uGUI contributes only reference concepts for adaptive layout and modular shell composition.
references.why: Use as the primary local summary for adaptive retained layout concepts built over IMGUI.

references.title: Unity Manual: IMGUI Layout Modes
references.interpretation: Use this to understand baseline IMGUI layout capabilities, not as the full framework architecture.
references.why: Use as the baseline on what IMGUI provides before the custom framework adds retained layout semantics.
